semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all twelve exact source mappings, proof-target schema and fixture, 298-target manifest and triage, 65-module/68-job Lean surface, 1,151-declaration proof-depth classification, 298-target readiness and adequacy classifications, wiring audit, blocked semantic-depth fixture and ten mutations, both proof targets, all seven ProofEnvelope declarations, every manifest atom, every prose candidate, test plan, source crosswalk, summary, and handoff. The sweep made the proposition-, predicate-, abstraction-, artifact-, verifier-, consumer-, implementation-, version-, environment-, and time-specific Formal Artifact Authority Lease the distinct owner; expanded it to eighteen mechanisms, twelve interfaces, eighteen invariants, and eighteen failure modes; preserved zero chapter-core effect; and froze the declaration-level proof-rationalization, richer-semantics, executable/empirical handoff, implementation-binding, independent-reproduction, and transfer campaign.

scope.population: Named prose propositions, proof targets, theorem declarations, schemas, executable models, formal artifacts, verifiers, consumers, implementations, environments, and releases seeking formal or executable authority.
scope.environment: Twelve exact mappings; one proof-target schema/fixture; 298 targets; 65 modules and 68 build jobs; 1,151 declarations classified by proof depth; readiness, traceability, and adequacy review surfaces; one blocked semantic-depth fixture plus ten mutations; two proof targets; seven local declarations. No complete stack semantics, deployed enforcement, general refinement, independent reproduction, or empirical result is represented.
scope.model: A proposition-, predicate-, abstraction-, artifact-, verifier-, consumer-, implementation-, version-, environment-, and time-specific lease over semantic adequacy, dependencies, verifier results, implementation binding, limitations, non-claims, expiry, revocation, and residuals.
scope.authority: This chapter owns formal-artifact adequacy and authorized interpretation only. Chapter owners, Claims/Evidence, Proof Transport, Schemas, Proof Tools, Runtime/Serving/Security, Benchmarks/Verification, Source/Artifact/Supply Chain, Resources, Readiness/Incident/Release, and Living-Book/Change Governance retain their boundaries.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-15; proposition, source, theorem, target, dependency, toolchain, specification, implementation, environment, policy, threat, rights, consumer, evidence, or time changes expire affected leases.
scope.artifact: Chapter and manifest; twelve mappings; proof schema and fixture; 298-target manifest/triage; 65-module/68-job build; 1,151-declaration depth report; readiness, traceability, and adequacy audits; semantic-depth harness; two targets; seven declarations; and future full rationalization/refinement campaign.

assumptions: Propositions, consumers, formal symbols, trusted bases, assumptions, dependencies, toolchains, implementations, environments, versions, and expiry can be observed and versioned.
Semantic adequacy can be reviewed against exact prose obligations, strongest counterclaims, reachable behavior, excluded cases, and consumer use without treating the review as proof.
Independent formalizations, checkers, executable models, implementations, evaluators, monitors, and adversarial schedules can expose shared errors and implementation gaps.
Failed builds, timeouts, solver unknowns, countermodels, mutations, flakiness, disputes, retired declarations, and unproved remainders remain visible.
Every activation-baseline target and declaration can receive a terminal disposition with lineage preserved.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: The proof corpus is mostly finite record discipline and traceability overhead; ordinary tests, reviews, and runtime controls provide more useful assurance, while theorem counts and green builds add confidence without semantic or deployed constraint.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: Declaration-level audit finds no consumer-bearing semantic value, or matched implementation and adversarial tests reveal vacuity, unreachable models, assumption restatement, missing behavior, broken refinement, stale consumers, excessive cost, revocation failure, or no independent reproduction/transfer; or executable and empirical lanes dominate without formal-artifact benefit.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze proof, specification, runtime-assurance, external-theorem, and formal-method comparators without importing reported results.
Justify consumer authority, least privilege, public wording, rights, revocation, retirement, and residual ownership.
Audit and build semantic, temporal, concurrency, noninterference, resource, recovery, probabilistic, countermodel, and refinement obligations where the prose requires them.
Run reference models, refinement checkers, mutations, adversarial schedules, runtime monitors, failures, revocation, fallback, and recovery.
Measure implementation conformance, runtime effects, monitor performance, proof/governance cost, false positives/negatives, safety, recovery, and residuals.
Compare formal-artifact consumers against tests/reviews alone and ablate assumptions, model details, monitors, and consumer gates under matched effort.
Reproduce with independent formalizations, tools, implementations, and evaluators across environments, consumers, attacks, organizations, and time.

contrary_evidence: All current ProofEnvelope declarations are finite record gates; two are direct projections and five are bounded negative cases.
The proof artifact audit establishes wiring only, not semantic necessity, source correctness, implementation binding, or deployed enforcement.
The semantic-depth fixture is blocked and synthetic; ten rejecting mutations demonstrate boundary checks, not arbitrary adequacy.
The adequacy report classifies only 13 of 298 targets as adequate finite records; most targets require narrower interpretation or richer formal, executable, empirical, or research lanes.
No chapter-core transition, complete declaration rationalization, independent formal reproduction, general refinement proof, runtime enforcement result, or transfer exists.
known_confounds: Hand-authored predicates, abstraction maps, assumptions, examples, consumers, and adequacy labels can encode the desired conclusion.
A consistent but incomplete model can hide concurrency, missing events, failures, adversaries, resources, rights, and open-world behavior.
Shared proof authors, kernels, tactics, formalizations, toolchains, implementations, traces, or evaluators can create false independence.
Theorem counts and syntactic depth can reward decomposition and proof style rather than semantic coverage or utility.
Formal and runtime-monitor cost can displace testing, modeling, operations, safety work, and recovery capacity.
acceptance_criteria: Every proposition and declaration has an owner, consumer, exact semantics, assumptions, dependencies, valid/invalid cases, artifact and verifier record, authority, expiry, revocation route, and residual owner.
All 298 targets and 1,151 baseline declarations receive terminal rationalization dispositions with lineage and zero unowned load-bearing obligations.
Load-bearing claims use adequate semantics, executable/refinement bridges, implementation binding, runtime observations, failures, and recovery rather than projection-only traceability.
Decisive claims survive countermodels, mutations, adversarial schedules, matched alternatives, independent formalizations/tools/implementations/evaluators, complete cost, and heterogeneous transfer.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact proposition, predicate, abstraction, assumptions, artifact, verifier, dependencies, consumer, implementation, environment, version, authority, and time.
Separate artifact existence, field presence, projection, finite route, derived invariant, executable bridge, implementation binding, observed effect, support, deployment, transfer, and SOTA.
Unknown semantic adequacy, implementation binding, cost, revocation, reproduction, transfer, rights, or open-world behavior remains residual or blocked.
refutation_criteria: Refute an artifact lease when vacuity, inconsistency, unreachability, hidden assumptions, semantic loss, failed verifier, broken dependency, wrong consumer, stale implementation, or revocation failure invalidates authority.

Refute formal-method benefit when matched tests/reviews/runtime controls equal or improve assurance and useful outcomes at lower complete cost, or the formal artifact does not constrain a real consumer.
Refute deployed enforcement, empirical truth, system safety, source correctness, support, transfer, or SOTA language when only finite formal or fixture evidence exists.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise formal-artifact owner supersedes the atom and every target, theorem, dependency, consumer, receipt, generated surface, implementation binding, release, residual, and revocation route migrates with lineage.

reproduction.command: python3 scripts/sync_proof_manifest.py --check && python3 scripts/validate_protocol_examples.py && python3 scripts/validate_proof_readiness.py && python3 scripts/validate_proof_artifact_audit.py && python3 scripts/validate_proof_depth_surface.py && python3 scripts/validate_proof_adequacy_review.py && python3 scripts/validate_formal_semantic_depth.py && cd lean && lake build AsiStackProofs.ProofEnvelope
reproduction.environment_lock: Local generated manifests, audits, fixtures, validators, and finite Lean module current through 2026-07-15; full declaration rationalization, runtime binding, independent formalization, and transfer not complete.
reproduction.evaluator_identity: Current generators, reviews, validators, and formalization are repository-local; independent formalizers, tools, implementations, runtime monitors, evaluators, attacks, and transfer environments are required.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Complete the declaration-level audit and execute required richer formal, executable, implementation-binding, empirical, causal, independent-reproduction, revocation, cost, and transfer lanes for every load-bearing claim.

non_claims: Counts, traceability, classifications, green builds, finite projections, and rejecting mutations do not prove semantic adequacy or the stack.
No deployed enforcement, complete implementation refinement, system safety, capability, performance, support promotion, independent reproduction, transfer, or SOTA result is represented.
External and historical-project sources provide design and contradiction context only, not imported local proof or runtime evidence.

executable-specifications-and-lean-proof.strongest_counterclaim: Formal record gates and theorem counts add overhead without constraining real consumers or improving assurance.
executable-specifications-and-lean-proof.falsifier: Full declaration and implementation audit reveals vacuity, no unique benefit, broken binding, excessive cost, revocation failure, or no independent reproduction/transfer.

executable-specifications-and-lean-proof.rationale: Semantically reviewed as bounded explanation, artifact or source report, lifecycle restatement, formal-surface description, scanner fragment, or explicit non-claim; the complete material obligation is already atomized and no independent promoted atom is created.

non_claims: Completing this semantic review does not prove the stack, promote support, or establish deployed enforcement, refinement, safety, capability, performance, transfer, or SOTA.
The 298-target and 1,151-declaration counts are inventory and classification facts, not semantic proof.
The future rationalization campaign is a required full-attempt specification, not a completed outcome.
